title: Mushroom Fruiting
description: A fungal colony seen in cross-section. Mycelium spreads through the substrate as a branching web of fine hyphae; where the web thickens, pins emerge and fruit into recognisable species — scarlet white-warted fly agaric, pot-bellied brown porcini, tall shaggy ink caps that deliquesce into black drips, clustered grey oyster shelves, golden wavy-edged chanterelles, and tiered turkey-tail bracket polypores on buried wood. Each grows, matures and drops drifting spores, then ages and withers while new flushes keep coming. Tick exactly which species you want to fruit. Click to inoculate a fresh flush; beats trigger a colony-wide flush. Growth speed, fruiting density, size, spore release, mycelium spread and substrate damp are live and mappable.
